scraped_bill_text.title: Fair Wage Act of 2016
scraped_bill_text.text: An act to amend Sections 1178 and 1182.12 of the Labor Code, and to amend Sections 445, 451, 452, 454, 457, 465, 470, and 472 of, and to add Sections 451.5 and 473 to, the Welfare and Institutions Code, relating to wages. SEC. 3. Section 1182.12 is added to the Labor Code, to read: 1182.12. (a) Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, the minimum wage for all industries shall not be less than the following: (1) Effective January 1, 2017, ten dollars ($10) per hour. (2) Effective January 1, 2018, ten dollars fifty cents ($10.50) per hour. (3) Effective January 1, 2019, eleven dollars ($11) per hour. (4) Effective January 1, 2020, twelve dollars ($12) per hour. (5) Effective January 1, 2021, thirteen dollars ($13) per hour. (6) Effective January 1, 2022, fourteen dollars ($14) per hour. (7) Effective January 1, 2023, fifteen dollars ($15) per hour.

rag_chunks.content: SB 3 (Fair Wage Act of 2016) gradually increases California's minimum wage from $10/hour in 2017 to $15/hour by 2023 for all industries.

web_sources.title: DOF Major Regulations Analysis - Minimum Wage
web_sources.snippet: SB 3 will increase wages for approximately 5.6 million workers. Estimated annual cost to employers: $22-29 billion.
